title: Drone and base station
abstract: A base station may include a parking plate on which the drone is to be landed. The base station may include pushers slidable on the parking plate. The pushers may be configured to push the legs of the drone to move the drone towards a reference location on the parking plate. The base station may include a gripper carried by the parking plate. The gripper may removably secure the body of the drone in place relative to the reference location. The base station may include an alignment sensor that detects whether a marker on the drone is in alignment with the reference location. A base station may also include a battery swapping system to replace the battery of the drone after the drone is secured.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A base station for receiving a drone comprising a body and a plurality of legs, the base station comprising: a parking plate on which the drone is to be landed; a plurality of pushers slidable on the parking plate, the plurality of pushers configured to push the legs of the drone to move the drone towards a reference location on the parking plate; a gripper carried by the parking plate, the gripper configured to removably secure the body of the drone in place relative to the reference location; and an alignment sensor carried by the parking plate and positioned at the reference location, the alignment sensor configured to detect whether a marker on the drone is in alignment with the reference location. 2. The base station of claim 1, wherein the gripper comprises two flaps, and each flap is configured to pivot from the parking plate to an erected position. 3. The base station of claim 1, wherein the gripper comprises a drone grip slot that is complementary with a tab protruding from the drone. 4. The base station of claim 1, wherein the marker on the drone is a QR code or another pattern configured to stand out as the marker. 5. The base station of claim 1, wherein the marker on the drone is an identifiable component of the drone. 6. The base station of claim 1, wherein each of the plurality of pushers have a plurality of leg notches that are configured to receive legs of the drone. 7. The base station of claim 1, wherein the plurality of pushers are configured to move along a set of tracks. 8. The base station of claim 1, wherein at least one of the pushers is an elongated bumper that comprises a tilted region, an orthogonal region, and a leg notch between the tilted region and the orthogonal region. 9. The base station of claim 1, further comprising a battery pack carrier configured to replace a battery pack carried by the drone. 10. The base station of claim 1, wherein the plurality of pushers are moveable between an expanded configuration and a contracted configuration that moves the drone towards the reference location. 11. The base station of claim 1, wherein parking plate forms a ceiling of a bottom compartment of the base station. 12. The base station of claim 11, wherein the bottom compartment carries one or more battery charging sites and a battery pack carrier. 13. The base station of claim 12, wherein the bottom compartment further carries a temperature regulator that is configured to regulate a temperature range of the bottom compartment. 14. The base station of claim 1, wherein the gripper comprises a detection port that is configured to detect whether the drone is secured with the gripper. 15. The base station of claim 14, wherein the detection port further comprises data transmission pins configured to download data from the drone to the base station. 16. The base station of claim 1, wherein the gripper is configured to secure the body of the drone subsequent to the alignment sensor detecting the marker on the drone being in alignment with the reference position. 17. A method for receiving a drone at a base station, the drone comprising a body and a plurality of legs, the method comprising: 

 receiving the drone on a parking plate of the base station; pushing a plurality of pushers that are slidable on the parking plate from an expanded configuration to a contracted configuration to push the legs of the drone to move the drone towards a reference location on the parking plate; using an alignment sensor to detect whether a marker on the drone is in alignment with the reference location, the alignment sensor carried by the parking plate and positioned at the reference location; and securing, using a gripper carried on the parking plate, the body of the drone in place relative to the reference location. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein securing, using the gripper, the body of the drone comprises pivoting from a flat configuration to an erected configuration. 19. The method of claim 17, further comprising swapping a battery pack of the drone after the drone is secured by the gripper. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein the gripper comprises a detection port that is configured to detect whether the drone is secured with the gripper and the detection port further comprises data transmission pins configured to download data from the drone to the base station. 21. A base station for performing a battery pack swap for a drone, the base station comprising: a parking plate for receiving the drone; a gripper carried by the parking plate, the gripper configured to secure the drone in place at a parking position at the parking plate; a plurality of chargers, each charger comprising a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged; and a battery pack carrier movable among the plurality of chargers and the parking position, the battery pack carrier configured to: 

 remove a first battery pack from the drone and move the first battery pack to a first charger, and carry a second battery pack from a second charger to the drone and install the second battery pack to the drone. 22. The base station of claim 21, wherein installing the second battery pack comprises: pushing the second battery pack up towards a bracket; and sliding in the second battery pack along a rail space created by the bracket. 23. The base station of claim 21, wherein the gripper comprises drone grip slots configured to complement with a portion of the drone to secure the drone. 24. The base station of claim 21, wherein the gripper comprises two flaps, and each flap is configured to pivot from the parking plate to an erected position. 25. The base station of claim 21, wherein the gripper comprises a charging port configured to provide power to the drone while the battery pack swap is performed 26. The base station of claim 21, wherein each charger further comprises a vertical sensor to determine that a battery pack has contacted a bracket, and a horizontal sensor to determine that the battery pack has slid into position to be charged. 27. The base station of claim 21, wherein the parking plate further comprises a shutter configured to open to provide the battery pack carrier with access to the drone. 28. The base station of claim 21, wherein the base station further comprises a retractable cover to protect the drone. 29. The base station of claim 21, wherein the battery pack carrier is movable along three axes. 30. The base station of claim 21, wherein the plurality of chargers are carried on a bottom surface of the parking plate. 31. The base station of claim 21, wherein the battery pack carrier comprises a track that is mounted on the parking plate. 32. The base station of claim 21, wherein the battery pack carrier comprises a battery latch prong configured to mate with a battery latch slot on the first battery pack. 33. A system comprising: a drone carrying a first battery pack; a base station for performing a battery pack swap for the drone, the base station comprising: a parking plate for receiving the drone; a gripper carried by the parking plate, the gripper configured to secure the drone in place at a parking position at the parking plate; a plurality of chargers, each charger comprising a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged; and a battery pack carrier movable among the plurality of chargers and the parking position, the battery pack carrier configured to: remove a first battery pack from the drone and move the first battery pack to a first charger, and carry a second battery pack from a second charger to the drone and install the second battery pack to the drone. 34. The system of claim 33, wherein the drone comprises a battery carrier that is configured to carry a battery pack. 35. The system of claim 34, wherein the battery carrier of the drone and a charger of the plurality of chargers carried by the base station have the same mechanical structure. 36. The system of claim 33, wherein the drone comprises a first data transmission port that is configured to be in contact with a second data transmission port carried on the gripper. 37. The system of claim 33, further comprising a second drone that is configured to use the base station for battery swap. 38. The system of claim 33, wherein the base station further comprises a temperature regulator configured to regulate a temperature range of a plurality of battery packs carried by the plurality of chargers. 39. A method for performing a battery pack swap for a drone that carries a first battery pack, the method comprising: holding, using a battery latch, a second battery pack in place with a charger carried by a base station; providing power to the second battery pack through a power port of the charger; receiving the drone on a parking plate of the base station; securing, using a gripper carried by the parking plate, the drone in place at a parking position at the parking plate; removing, using a battery pack carrier, the first battery pack from the drone, wherein the battery pack carrier is movable between the charger and the parking position; carrying, using the battery pack carrier, from the charger to the drone; and installing the second battery pack to the drone. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein installing the second battery pack to the drone comprises: pushing the second battery pack up towards a bracket; and sliding in the second battery pack along a rail space created by the bracket. 41. A base station for performing a battery pack swap for a drone, the base station comprising: 

 a frame configured to provide mechanical support to the base station; a parking plate carried by the frame, the parking plate comprising a landing surface configured to be in contact with the drone when the drone is landed and a component-carrying surface opposing the landing surface; a plurality of chargers carried on the component-carrying surface of the parking plate; a battery pack carrier carried on the component-carrying surface of the parking plate, the battery pack carrier movable to carry a battery pack connected to one of the chargers to the drone; and a shutter on the parking plate, the shutter openable to provide access of the battery pack carrier from the component-carrying surface to the landing surface. 42. The base station of claim 41, wherein the component-carrying surface forms a ceiling of a bottom compartment of the base station, and the plurality of chargers and the battery pack carrier are carried inside the bottom compartment. 43. The base station of claim 41, wherein each charger comprises a vertical sensor to determine that a battery pack has contacted a bracket, and a horizontal sensor to determine that the battery pack has slid into position to be charged. 44. The base station of claim 41, wherein the battery pack carrier includes a robotic arm. 45. The base station of claim 41, wherein each charger comprises a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged. 46. The base station of claim 41, wherein the battery pack carrier is configured to install a battery pack on the drone by: pushing the battery pack up towards a bracket; and sliding in the battery pack along a rail space created by the bracket. 47. The base station of claim 41, wherein the battery pack carrier comprises a lateral track, a longitudinal track, and a vertical track, and one of the tracks is mounted on the component-carrying surface of the parking plate. 48. The base station of claim 41, wherein the battery pack carrier is movable along three axes. 49. The base station of claim 41, wherein the base station further comprises a gripper configured to removably secure the drone, and wherein the gripper further comprises a power port to power the drone while the battery pack carrier is swapping battery packs. 50. The base station of claim 49, wherein the gripper further comprises drone grip slots configured to complement with a portion of the drone to secure the drone. 51. The base station of claim 41, wherein the plurality of chargers are carried on a flip side of the landing surface on which the drone is landed. 52. The base station of claim 41, wherein the battery pack carrier is movable among the plurality of chargers and the shutter. 53. The base station of claim 41, wherein the plurality of chargers are arranged linearly. 54. The base station of claim 41, wherein walls carried by the frame and the parking plate define an enclosure for the plurality of chargers, and the base station comprises a temperature regulator to regulate a temperature range of the enclosure. 55. The system comprising: a drone carrying a battery pack; and a base station for performing a battery pack swap for the drone, the base station comprising: 

 a frame configured to provide mechanical support to the base station; a parking plate carried by the frame, the parking plate comprising a landing surface configured to be in contact with the drone when the drone is landed and a component-carrying surface opposing the landing surface; a plurality of chargers carried on the component-carrying surface of the parking plate; a battery pack carrier carried on the component-carrying surface of the parking plate, the battery pack carrier movable to carry a battery pack connected to one of the chargers to the drone; and a shutter on the parking plate, the shutter openable to provide access of the battery pack carrier from the component-carrying surface to the landing surface. 56. The system of claim 55, wherein the component-carrying surface forms a ceiling of a bottom compartment of the base station, and the plurality of chargers and the battery pack carrier are carried inside the bottom compartment. 57. The system of claim 55, wherein each charger comprises a vertical sensor to determine that a battery pack has contacted a bracket, and a horizontal sensor to determine that the battery pack has slid into position to be charged. 58. The system of claim 55, wherein each charger comprises a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged. 59. The system of claim 55, wherein the battery pack carrier is configured to install a battery pack on the drone by: pushing the battery pack up towards a bracket; and sliding in the battery pack along a rail space created by the bracket. 60. The system of claim 55, wherein the plurality of chargers are carried on a flip side of the landing surface on which the drone is landed. 61. A base station for receiving a drone, the base station comprising: a cabinet comprising a parking plate and one or more walls forming an enclosure for one or more internal components of the base station, the parking plate configured to receive the drone; a plurality of chargers carried within the enclosure, each charger configured to provide power to a battery pack being charged at the charger; a temperature sensor carried within the enclosure, the temperature sensor configured to measure a temperature within the enclosure; and a temperature regulator configured to regulate the temperature within the enclosure to maintain the temperature of a plurality of battery packs charged at the plurality of chargers within a temperature range. 62. The base station of claim 61, wherein each charger further comprises a vertical sensor to determine that a battery pack has contacted a bracket, and a horizontal sensor to determine that the battery pack has slid into position to be charged. 63. The base station of claim 61, further comprising a battery pack carrier carried within an enclosure, the battery pack carrier configured to carry a battery pack to the drone. 64. The base station of claim 61, wherein the temperature regulator is an air mover. 65. The base station of claim 61, wherein the base station comprises a battery pack carrier configured to swap battery packs between the plurality of chargers and the drone. 66. The base station of claim 61, wherein each charger comprises a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged. 67. The base station of claim 61, wherein the parking plate further comprises a shutter configured to protect the enclosure, and the shutter openable to provide access to the plurality of chargers. 68. The base station of claim 61, wherein the parking plate includes a landing surface and a component-carrying surface opposing the landing surface, and the component-carrying surface is facing the enclosure. 69. The base station of claim 68, wherein the plurality of the chargers are carried on the component-carrying surface. 70. The base station of claim 61, wherein the temperature regulator includes a cooling component and a heating component. 71. A method for regulating temperature of drone battery packs, the method comprising: enclosing a plurality of chargers carried within an enclosure formed from a parking plate and one or more walls of a base station that is configured to receive a drone; providing power to a battery pack that is being charged one of the charger; measuring a temperature within the enclosure of the base station using a temperature sensor carried within the enclosure; and regulating, using a temperature regulator, the temperature with the enclosure to maintain the temperature of a plurality of battery packs charged at the plurality of chargers within a temperature range. 72. The method of claim 71, wherein each charger further comprises a vertical sensor to determine that a battery pack has contacted a bracket, and a horizontal sensor to determine that the battery pack has slid into position to be charged. 73. The method of claim 71, further comprising performing a battery swap for the drone. 74. The method of claim 71, wherein the temperature regulator is an air mover. 75. The method of claim 71, wherein the base station comprises a battery pack carrier configured to swap battery packs between the plurality of chargers and the drone. 76. The method of claim 71, wherein each charger comprises a battery latch and a power port, the battery latch configured to mechanically hold a battery pack in place with the charger and the power port configured to provide power to the battery pack being charged. 77. The method of claim 71, wherein the parking plate further comprises a shutter configured to protect the enclosure, and the shutter openable to provide access to the plurality of chargers. 78. The method of claim 71, wherein the parking plate includes a landing surface and a component-carrying surface opposing the landing surface, and the componentcarrying surface is facing the enclosure. 79. The method of claim 78, wherein the plurality of the chargers are carried on the component-carrying surface. 80. The method of claim 71, wherein the temperature regulator includes a cooling component and a heating component. 81. An aerial drone, comprising: a drone body having a longitudinal housing carrying a processing circuit and a battery, the longitudinal housing extending in a first direction; a sensor rod carried by the drone body and extending from the drone body in a second direction different from the first direction, the sensor rod carrying a sensor at a distal end of the sensor rod; and 

 a propeller guard connected to the distal end of the sensor rod and supported at least partially by the sensor rod, the propeller guard forming part of a periphery of the aerial drone. 82. The aerial drone of claim 81, wherein the drone body and the sensor rod are perpendicular and create multiple external points for the periphery of the aerial drone. 83. The aerial drone of claim 82, wherein a depth sensor is carried at each of the multiple external points of the aerial drone. 84. The aerial drone of claim 81, further comprising a plurality of bottom depth sensors carried by the longitudinal housing, at least one of the bottom depth sensors is arranged in a tilted configuration. 85. The aerial drone of claim 81, further comprising: a gimbal carried by one end of the longitudinal housing, the gimbal configured to support a camera. 86. The aerial drone of claim 81, wherein the sensor rod carries two sensors, one at each end of the sensor rod. 87. The aerial drone of claim 81, further comprising: one or more motor rods carried by the drone body, each motor rod configured to carry a propeller. 88. The aerial drone of claim 87, wherein the longitudinal housing comprises a first housing part and a second housing part, and the sensor rod is connected to the first housing part and the motor rods are connected to the second housing part. 89. The aerial drone of claim 87, further comprising: a landing leg carried by one of the motor rods, the landing leg attached to a leg tip for absorbing impact of landing 90. The aerial drone of claim 81, further comprising: one or more gripper tabs carried by the drone body, the gripper tabs configured to interact with a base station to align the drone. 91. The aerial drone of claim 90, further comprising: a communication port configured to provide detection of whether the one or more gripper tabs are gripped by a gripper of the base station. 92. The aerial drone of claim 91, wherein the communication port is further configured to transmit power to the aerial drone and transmit data from the aerial drone to the base station. 93. The aerial drone of claim 81, wherein the propeller guard connects to the sensor rod and an end of the longitudinal housing. 94. The aerial drone of claim 93, wherein the propeller guard balloons out from the sensor rod and the end of the longitudinal housing supports. 95. The aerial drone of claim 81, wherein the longitudinal housing comprises a first housing part and a second housing part that are divided through a plane and the two housing parts are connected through a plurality of connection sites that are in parallel of the plane. 96. The aerial drone of claim 81, further comprising: one or more sensors coupled to a bottom surface of the drone body; one or more sensors coupled to a top surface of the drone body. 97. The aerial drone of claim 95, further comprising: for each sensor, a heat sink carried by the sensor. 98. A system comprising: a base station for performing battery swap operation; and 

 an aerial drone, comprising: a drone body having a longitudinal housing carrying a processing circuit and a battery, the longitudinal housing extending in a first direction; a sensor rod carried by the drone body and extending from the drone body in a second direction different from the first direction, the sensor rod carrying a sensor at a distal end of the sensor rod; and a propeller guard connected to the distal end of the sensor rod and supported at least partially by the sensor rod, the propeller guard forming part of a periphery of the aerial drone. 99. The system of claim 98, wherein the base station comprises a gripper, the drone comprises one or more gripper tabs carried by the drone body, and the gripper tabs are configured to interact with a base station to align the drone 100. The system of claim 99, wherein the aerial drone further comprises: a communication port configured to provide detection of whether the one or more gripper tabs are gripped by a gripper of the base station. 101. An aerial drone, comprising: a drone body for carrying a battery pack; a slide guide carried by the drone body, wherein the slide guide suspends from a surface of the drone body and creates a channel between the slide guide and the surface, and wherein the battery pack is slidable along the channel; a slide-guide contact sensor carried on the surface of the drone body, the slideguide contact sensor configured to detect whether the battery pack is in contact with the surface; a connection port carried by the drone body; and a port contact sensor carried by the drone body, the port contact sensor is configured to detect whether the battery pack is slid in to the connection port. 102. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the slide-guide contact sensor comprises a proximity spring configured to push a pin and break a circuit in response to compression by the battery pack. 103. The aerial drone of claim 101, further comprising a communication port that is configured to transmit power to the aerial drone and download data from the aerial drone. 104. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the slide-guide contact sensor and the port contact sensor are arranged in two different angles to detect contact of the battery pack in two directions. 105. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the slide guide is a bracket that is made of a metal or a plastic. 106. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the slide guide is shaped to include a series of alternating slots and ledges. 107. The aerial drone of claim 106, wherein the battery pack comprises tabs that are complementary to the slots of the slide guide. 108. The aerial drone of claim 107, wherein the ledges are configured to support at least part of the weight of the battery pack through holding the tabs of the battery pack when the battery pack is connected to the connection port. 109. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the battery pack comprises: a temperature sensor carried inside a housing of the battery pack, the temperature sensor configured to monitor the temperature of the battery pack. 110. The aerial drone of claim 101, wherein the battery pack comprises: a PCB board configured to monitor a charge level of the battery pack. 111. The aerial drone of claim 101, further comprising a battery latch that is spring loaded. 112. The aerial drone of claim 111, wherein the slide guide is configured to prevent the battery pack from moving in a first direction and the battery latch is configured to prevent battery pack from moving in a second direction. 113. A system comprising: a base station comprising a battery pack carrier; and an aerial drone, the aerial drone comprising: a drone body for carrying a battery pack; a slide guide carried by the drone body, wherein the slide guide suspends from a surface of the drone body and creates a channel between the slide guide and the surface, and wherein the battery pack is slidable along the channel; a slide-guide contact sensor carried on the surface of the drone body, the slide-guide contact sensor configured to detect whether the battery pack is in contact with the surface; a connection port carried by the drone body; and a port contact sensor carried by the drone body, the port contact sensor is configured to detect whether the battery pack is slid in to the connection port. 114. The system of claim 113, wherein the battery pack carrier is configured to perform a battery swap operation, and the battery swap operation comprises: moving the battery pack towards the slide-guide contact sensor; sliding the battery pack through the channel; and connecting the battery pack to the connection port. 115. The system of claim 113, wherein the aerial drone further comprises a battery latch that is spring loaded, the slide guide is configured to prevent the battery pack from moving in a first direction and the battery latch is configured to prevent battery pack from moving in a second direction 116. The system of claim 113, wherein the aerial drone comprises a communication port that is configured to transmit power to the aerial drone and download data from the aerial drone to the base station. 117. A method for installing a battery pack comprising a plurality of tabs to an aerial drone, the method comprising: aligning the tabs of the battery pack to slots of a slide guide carried by a drone body of the aerial drone, wherein the slide guide suspends from a surface of the drone body and creates a channel between the slide guide and the surface; pushing the battery pack towards the drone body of the aerial drone; detecting, by a slide-guide contact sensor of the aerial drone, that the battery pack is in contact with the slide-guide contact sensor; and sliding the battery pack towards a connection port through the channel. 118. The method of claim 117, further comprising: locking the battery pack in place with the connection port using a battery latch. 119. The method of claim 118, wherein the slide guide is configured to prevent the battery pack from moving in a first direction and the battery latch is configured to prevent battery pack from moving in a second direction. 120. The method of claim 117, wherein the slide guide is shaped to include a series of alternating slots and ledges and when the battery pack is connected to the connection port, the ledges are configured to support at least part of the weight of the battery pack through holding the tabs of the battery pack.
